authoritative_sources.title: HIPAA Minimum Necessary Standard
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health & Human Services, Office for Civil Rights
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HHS OCR is the federal HIPAA privacy regulator. The guidance is directly authoritative for limiting patient/member identifiers, service addresses, diagnoses, equipment notes, and vendor communications to the minimum necessary.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: PHI-limited parts sourcing, vendor communication, shipping, and stakeholder routing

authoritative_sources.title: 42 CFR 424.57 - Special payment rules for items furnished by DMEPOS suppliers and issuance of DMEPOS supplier billing privileges
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary federal regulatory text for DMEPOS supplier standards, billing privileges, order, delivery, and supplier-operation conditions. It is authoritative for DME/HME parts boundaries in care-adjacent home services.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: DME/HME supplier, replacement, delivery, and documentation prerequisite handling

authoritative_sources.title: DMEPOS Quality Standards
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS quality standards are authoritative operating guidance for DMEPOS supplier service, delivery, setup, patient instruction, complaint, and record expectations relevant to parts and replacement readiness.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: DME/HME delivery, setup, service, complaint, and operational support evidence
used_for: patient instruction and complaint routing boundaries

authoritative_sources.title: CMS Interoperability and Prior Authorization Final Rule
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS prior authorization guidance is authoritative for payer/provider authorization workflow expectations and supports escalation when parts procurement is blocked by authorization, payer-data, referral, or eligibility status.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: authorization, referral, eligibility, and payer prerequisite handling
used_for: patient access and utilization management routing

authoritative_sources.title: United States Core Data for Interoperability Version 6
authoritative_sources.publisher: Assistant Secretary for Technology Policy / Office of the National Coordinator for Health IT
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ASTP/ONC USCDI is the federal healthcare interoperability data baseline. It supports patient, encounter, order, care team, procedure, clinical note, and device terminology used in EHR, CRM, and operational parts records.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: patient/member, order, encounter, device, and source-of-truth terminology normalization

authoritative_sources.title: Unique Device Identification System
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA is the federal medical-device regulator. UDI guidance is authoritative for device identifier, model, lot, serial, and traceability concepts when home-service parts include DME/HME or other medical devices.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: medical-device identifier, model, lot, serial, and traceability handling

authoritative_sources.title: Stationary Refrigeration and Air Conditioning
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: EPA administers Clean Air Act Section 608 rules for refrigerant handling, recovery, certification, and service practices. This is authoritative when HVAC parts procurement touches refrigerant-controlled work.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: HVAC refrigerant, certification, recovery, and safety-boundary handling

authoritative_sources.title: 29 CFR 1910.1200 - Hazard communication
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary OSHA hazard-communication regulation for chemical classification, labels, and safety data sheets. It constrains sourcing, storage, technician handoff, and escalation for hazardous service parts.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: hazardous-material label, SDS, storage, and field handoff handling

authoritative_sources.title: Recalls
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CPSC is the federal consumer product safety regulator. Recall data is authoritative for identifying consumer product recall and stop-use constraints affecting appliance, home-service, and replacement parts.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: recall, stop-use, product-safety hold, and customer-risk escalation

authoritative_sources.title: 16 CFR Part 429 - Rule Concerning Cooling-off Period for Sales Made at Homes or at Certain Other Locations
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary federal trade regulation for covered in-home sales and cancellation rights. It is authoritative when parts procurement or replacement handling implicates in-home agreement, cancellation, or service-sale posture.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: agreement, cancellation, and home-service legal-boundary handling

workflow_questions.question: What prerequisites or authorizations gate the workflow?
workflow_questions.answer: Required prerequisites include tenant service model, jurisdiction, business unit, product category, source hierarchy, approval matrix, RACI, escalation SLA, work order, service date, requested part, model, serial, installed equipment, warranty or recall posture, approved vendor or substitute rule, inventory or vendor status, technician note, shipment or ETA evidence, privacy constraints, and, when healthcare linked, patient/member status, payer, plan, benefits, eligibility, referral, authorization, order, encounter, discharge or care-transition note, documentation state, and minimum-necessary handling rule. Missing authorization, referral, eligibility, order, approved source, recall/safety hold, hazardous-material controls, or technician-readiness evidence blocks resolution.

workflow_questions.question: Which records or systems establish the patient, member, or claim truth set?
workflow_questions.answer: Truth-set systems include EHR/EMR, clinical documentation system, practice management, revenue cycle, payer portal, eligibility system, authorization or utilization management system, referral management, case management, CRM, field-service management or work-order system, dispatch system, inventory or warehouse system, ERP or purchasing system, vendor portal, warranty system, recall or product-safety repository, technician mobile notes, document repository, privacy/access-control system, and audit logs. Claim truth remains with revenue cycle, coding, payer operations, or utilization management rather than this lane.

workflow_questions.question: Where does the lane stop and licensed clinical, privacy, or payer-interpretation authority begin?
workflow_questions.answer: The lane stops before diagnosis, treatment planning, clinical need, medical necessity, care-pathway decisions, licensed review, DME/HME clinical setup decisions, patient-safety signoff, PHI disclosure approval, consent interpretation, payer coverage, authorization approval, referral sufficiency, claim/coding/denial/appeal decisions, legal agreement interpretation, vendor onboarding, substitute-part approval, spend approval, recall remedy decisions, hazardous-material release, licensed trade signoff, technician execution, and safety-critical release.

workflow_stages.name: Parts support intake and boundary classification
workflow_stages.description: Capture requested part action, service model, jurisdiction, work order, product category, customer or patient/member context, source systems, RACI, approval matrix, escalation SLA, privacy posture, and authority posture before accepting work.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance

workflow_stages.name: Prerequisite and evidence validation
workflow_stages.description: Validate work order, service date, model, serial, SKU, lot, installed equipment, technician note, inventory state, approved source, substitute rule, PO, vendor ETA, warranty, recall, SDS, refrigerant, authorization, referral, eligibility, order, encounter, privacy posture, and source-of-truth hierarchy.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance
Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: Availability, sourcing, and vendor-risk support
workflow_stages.description: Reconcile stock, truck stock, transfer, PO, vendor quote, backorder, RMA, shipment, ETA, approved-source, substitute, recall, and service-impact records; produce a status summary or vendor-risk escalation based on auditable evidence.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance
Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: Stakeholder communication and handoff
workflow_stages.description: Prepare customer, patient/member-safe, vendor, technician, or internal stakeholder communication summaries limited to supported procurement facts, then hand off clinical, payer, privacy, legal, safety, licensed, warranty, supplier, finance, or scheduling decisions.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance

workflow_stages.name: Exception, shortage, recall, and safety handling
workflow_stages.description: Escalate missing prerequisites, conflicting systems, repeat vendor failures, product recalls, stop-use notices, refrigerant controls, SDS or label gaps, hazardous-material requirements, policy exceptions, and unsupported workarounds to accountable owners.

workflow_stages.name: Resolution confirmation and evidence capture
workflow_stages.description: Record resolution only when request disposition, source records, inventory/vendor state, prerequisite posture, communication summary, owner acceptance, residual risk, and reopen trigger are documented.
source_classes: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
Public payer or provider operating guidance

artifacts: service ticket, CRM case, work order, dispatch record, service date, service address, and technician mobile note
requested part, SKU, model, serial, lot, UDI or device identifier, installed equipment record, substitute matrix, and approved-source record

inventory bin, warehouse stock, truck stock, reservation, transfer order, purchase requisition, purchase order, vendor quote, backorder notice, shipment tracking, receiving record, return authorization, and vendor portal note
warranty status, recall status, stop-use notice, product-safety hold, SDS, hazardous-material label, refrigerant-handling constraint, and safety escalation record
eligibility, benefits, referral, authorization, order, encounter, discharge or care-transition, and clinical-administrative notes
charge capture, coding, claim, remittance, denial, appeal, and payer portal references used only as boundary or routing evidence

privacy-controlled routing note, parts prerequisite checklist, availability status summary, shortage or vendor-risk escalation note, stakeholder communication summary, resolution evidence record, and audit log
systems_of_record: technician mobile application or field note repository
inventory, warehouse, truck-stock, or bin-control system
ERP, purchasing, procurement, or purchase-order system
vendor portal, supplier catalog, approved supplier list, quote, backorder, shipment, receiving, or RMA system
warranty system and recall or product-safety repository
EHR or EMR and clinical documentation systems
revenue cycle, charge capture, coding, claim, remittance, denial, or appeal system

allowed: Classify parts and procurement support requests as in scope, out of scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites.
Request minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, source links, approved-source context, and owner context needed to continue safely.
Resolve supported status requests by summarizing documented stock, vendor, PO, ETA, shipping, warranty, recall, authorization, referral, eligibility, privacy, and work-order evidence.

Prepare service-support dispositions, availability or logistics summaries, customer or stakeholder communication summaries, parts prerequisite checklists, vendor-risk or shortage escalation notes, and resolution evidence records.
Route clinical, payer, privacy, legal, supplier, spend, warranty, safety, licensed trade, technician, scheduling, coding, revenue cycle, and compliance decisions to adjacent owners.
escalate: Required work-order, model, serial, inventory, approved-vendor, substitute, warranty, recall, SDS, refrigerant, EHR/EMR, payer, authorization, referral, eligibility, privacy, or tenant authority records are missing or materially conflict.

The request requires clinical judgment, medical necessity, payer interpretation, PHI disclosure approval, coding, claim, denial, appeal, agreement/legal interpretation, vendor onboarding, spend approval, substitute approval, warranty remedy, licensed trade signoff, technician execution, hazardous-material release, or safety-critical release.
Public guidance, tenant policy, payer rules, vendor terms, product recall status, hazardous-material controls, state cancellation posture, agreement terms, or system records conflict materially.
A patient-safety, PHI, DME/HME, recall, site-safety, technician-readiness, refrigerant, SDS, hazardous-material, or product-safety signal changes whether support can continue.
refuse: Mark parts support resolved without documented disposition, inventory or vendor status, prerequisite evidence, owner acceptance, and record links.

Approve substitute parts, unapproved vendors, spend exceptions, warranty remedies, refunds, agreement waivers, cancellation outcomes, payer coverage, authorization approval, medical necessity, coding, claim handling, PHI disclosure, or clinical recommendations.

Release or source a part affected by recall, stop-use, safety hold, hazardous-material rule, refrigerant handling rule, missing SDS, approved-source conflict, or unresolved patient-safety signal without accountable owner review.
Authorize field service or technician execution when licensed trade, safety, infection-control, scope, access, or site-readiness blockers require accountable owner review.

domain_failure_modes: parts support proceeds without authorization, referral, eligibility, order, agreement, service-date, model, serial, inventory, vendor, or work-order prerequisites
patient/member PHI is included in broad vendor sourcing or ETA communications without minimum-necessary controls
the lane implies medical necessity, diagnosis, treatment planning, clinical need, or payer coverage while only owning administrative procurement support
a substitute part, unapproved vendor, spend exception, warranty remedy, or policy workaround is approved by the support lane without accountable authority
recall, stop-use, refrigerant, SDS, hazardous-material, DME/HME setup, patient-safety, or product-safety blockers are treated as ordinary availability issues
conflicting CRM, FSM, inventory, ERP, vendor, warranty, recall, technician, EHR, or payer records are synthesized into unsupported resolution
repeat vendor failure is communicated as a firm ETA without source evidence or service-impact escalation
tenant terminology is mapped casually and changes the meaning of NLA, RMA, BO, DME swap, authorization pending, referral missing, vendor hold, or technician no-go

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Missing authorization, referral, or eligibility dependency
scenario_family_coverage.coverage_note: Payer-funded DME/HME replacement is blocked until eligibility, referral, authorization, and order prerequisites are resolved by patient access or utilization management.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: PHI or privacy-sensitive request that requires controlled handling
scenario_family_coverage.coverage_note: Broad vendor sourcing request containing patient identifiers, diagnosis, address, and equipment notes is refused or constrained under minimum-necessary handling.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would require licensed clinical or payer-interpretation escalation
scenario_family_coverage.coverage_note: Medical necessity, payer coverage, payer appeal, coding, and clinical replacement-need decisions are escalated rather than handled by parts procurement.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Customer or stakeholder requests a policy exception
scenario_family_coverage.coverage_note: Approved-vendor, spend-limit, substitute, recall, and SLA conflicts trigger refusal or escalation rather than discretionary exception handling.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Service request lacks the detail needed for safe resolution
scenario_family_coverage.coverage_note: Missing work-order, model, serial, tenant authority, approved substitute, and retrieval data blocks action and requires a facts checklist.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Communication becomes a handoff to another lane
scenario_family_coverage.coverage_note: Stakeholder communication is limited to procurement facts while clinical review, payer appeal, coding, warranty, supplier, scheduling, and technician requests are handed off.
Second missed vendor ETA and conflicting FSM readiness are handled as vendor-risk escalation with service impact and owner routing.
Recall, tenant vendor policy, payer authorization, vendor substitution, and service SLA conflict is escalated without synthesis.
Tenant abbreviations and operational terms are normalized while preserving assumptions and escalation for missing definitions.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: State-specific contractor, appliance, DME/HME, and in-home service rules
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_policy: Require jurisdiction, tenant legal/compliance policy, provider type, service setting, and program context before final disposition; escalate legal or compliance conflicts.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific approved substitute, vendor, warranty, and spend authority
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_policy: Require approved-source matrix, vendor policy, warranty policy, spend approval matrix, and RACI before ordering or recommending exceptions.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Payer-specific coverage, authorization, and documentation rules
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_policy: Use public CMS guidance only as a baseline; require payer, plan, authorization, referral, order, and provider records and route interpretation to patient access, utilization management, or payer operations.

market_ready_research_conclusion.summary: The public baseline is sufficient for a validated specification-based package because it defines DME/HME, PHI, authorization, device-identifier, recall, refrigerant, hazardous-material, home-service, and communication boundaries while requiring tenant-specific retrieval and escalation for operational decisions.
